summary:
The primary consideration when choosing a laser for TMJ adhesion lysis via arthroscopic procedure involves minimizing heat-induced complications while ensuring precise incisions; hence, Holmium Yttrium-Aluminum Garnet (HO:YAG) remains preferred due its ability to achieve this balance effectively compared to alternatives like Erbium-doped yttrium aluminum garnet (ER:YAG).

The summary provided focuses more broadly on comparing different types of lasers rather than directly addressing why specifically an HO:YAG laser would be chosen over others based solely upon requirements from the initial query about selecting between specific options given no additional context or comparison beyond what was initially stated as "the laser used". Let me refine it accordingly focusing strictly per your instructions towards identifying core reasons behind selection within constraints set forth originally concerning only one type being selected among specified choices available at start point here i.e., either 'laser' option presented versus another not mentioned explicitly but implied implicitly elsewhere outside scope defined clearly just above where you asked succinctly summarized rationale leading up final conclusion reached after analysis performed."

In arthroscopy for treating temporomandibular joint (TMJ) adhesions, using a holmium-yttrium-aluminum-garnet (Holmium:YAG) laser minimizes unwanted heating near critical structures such as nerves because it produces less thermal energy relative to alternative diode-based systems commonly considered. This characteristic enhances safety margins crucial especially close anatomical regions prone to nerve irritation post-surgery involving jaw movement mechanics affected indirectly thereby supporting
